raw_text:
Manager, MLOps Engineer

VectorFlow AI — Democratizing machine learning operations at enterprise scale

Location

San Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly)

Employment Type

Full-time

Compensation

$210,000 - $270,000 + equity

The Opportunity

Join VectorFlow AI as Manager, MLOps Engineer and lead a team of 5-7 engineers responsible for

building and scaling our core MLOps infrastructure. In this role, you'll oversee the architecture and

deployment of ML workflows supporting hundreds of production models, manage a high-performing

team, mentor engineers on MLOps best practices, and drive strategic initiatives around model

observability and platform reliability. You'll work closely with product, data science, and infrastructure

teams to ensure our platform delivers exceptional performance and reliability to enterprise customers.

About VectorFlow AI

VectorFlow AI is a Series B-funded MLOps platform company dedicated to helping enterprises
streamline their machine learning infrastructure, model deployment, and production monitoring. We
serve 200+ companies across financial services, healthcare, and e-commerce, enabling data teams to

go from experimentation to production in days, not months. Our platform integrates seamlessly with
existing data stacks and provides end-to-end visibility into ML systems.

Day-to-Day

Lead and manage a team of 5-7 MLOps engineers, including hiring, performance reviews, career

development, and fostering a culture of technical excellence and collaboration

Oversee the design and implementation of scalable ML infrastructure using Kubernetes, Docker,
and cloud-native technologies to support 500+ production models across customer deployments

Drive architectural decisions for workflow orchestration using Apache Airflow and Kubeflow,
ensuring reliability, scalability, and ease of use for data teams

Establish and enforce MLOps best practices, including CI/CD pipelines for model deployment,
automated testing frameworks, and infrastructure-as-code standards using GitOps principles

Build and maintain comprehensive model monitoring and governance solutions using MLflow,
Prometheus, and custom observability tools to detect model drift, performance degradation, and
data quality issues

Partner with the Data Engineering team to integrate feature stores and manage feature pipelines
that serve both training and inference workflows at scale

Identify and execute on strategic technical roadmap items; propose and lead engineering initiatives
that improve platform reliability, reduce operational toil, and enhance customer experience

Conduct regular 1-on-1s with direct reports, provide technical mentorship, and support team

members in advancing their technical skills and career growth

 Represent the MLOps team in cross-functional discussions with product, sales, and customer

success to translate business requirements into technical roadmap priorities

You Have

6+ years of experience in MLOps, platform engineering, or machine learning infrastructure, with at

least 2+ years in a management or tech lead role

Demonstrated expertise building and operating production ML systems at scale, including model

training pipelines, serving infrastructure, and monitoring systems

Deep hands-on experience with at least two of: Apache Airflow, Kubeflow, or similar orchestration

platforms; and proficiency with Docker and Kubernetes for containerized ML workloads

Strong software engineering practices including version control, testing, CI/CD automation, and

infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, CloudFormation, or similar)

Proven track record managing and mentoring engineering teams, with experience in hiring,

performance management, and creating psychological safety within teams

Solid understanding of ML lifecycle concepts including model evaluation, hyperparameter

optimization, model registry/versioning (e.g., MLflow), and model governance

Experience with monitoring, observability, and alerting for ML systems; familiarity with tools like
Prometheus, Grafana, or equivalent platforms

Bonus

Experience building or maintaining a feature store solution or working closely with feature
engineering platforms (e.g., Tecton, Feast, or internal solutions)

Exposure to model monitoring frameworks and tools for detecting model drift, data drift, and
prediction quality degradation

Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and their managed ML services (SageMaker,
Vertex AI, etc.)

Background in data engineering or data infrastructure that complements MLOps knowledge

Experience with open-source MLOps tools and active contribution to or leadership in the MLOps
community

Our Stack

Kubernetes   Docker   Apache Airﬂow   Kubeﬂow   MLﬂow   Prometheus   Python   Git / GitHub   Terraform

Feature Stores (Feast / Tecton)

What We Offer

Competitive salary and equity package; 401(k) matching (up to 4%)

Comprehensive health coverage including medical, dental, vision, and mental health support

Unlimited PTO with a minimum of 15 days encouraged; 10 company holidays

Professional development budget ($3,000/year for courses, conferences, and certifications)

Remote-friendly culture with co-working stipend and option for office space in SF, NYC, or Seattle

Parental leave (16 weeks primary caregiver, 12 weeks secondary caregiver) and fertility benefits

 Home office setup allowance and annual equipment refresh budget

Team off-sites and regular social events; commuter benefits and gym membership reimbursement

Apply

Apply directly via our careers page at vectorflow.ai/careers or email your resume and a brief cover

letter explaining your interest in MLOps leadership to careers@vectorflow.ai. We review applications

on a rolling basis and aim to respond to all candidates within 2 weeks. Interviews will include a

technical discussion, system design conversation, and team meeting with current MLOps leaders.
